ABCD_SubMod_StatAnalysis

Felicia Hardi

2025-07-25

Load packages

library(broom)
library(cluster)
library(dplyr)
library(emmeans)
library(factoextra)
library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
library(Hmisc)
library(interactions)
library(kableExtra)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(knitr)
library(magrittr)
library(NbClust)
library(psych)
library(purrr)
library(table1)
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

Create dfs

agnes_res_env$clust = as.factor(agnes_res_env$clust)
agnes_res_env$mri_info_manufacturer = as.factor(agnes_res_env$mri_info_manufacturer)
agnes_res_env$site_id_l = as.factor(agnes_res_env$site_id_l)
agnes_res_env$sex = agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2
agnes_res_env$sex = ifelse(agnes_res_env$sex==3, NA, agnes_res_env$sex)
agnes_res_env$sex = as.factor(agnes_res_env$sex)

# bpm long (6 timepoints)
# create long version of bpm
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = 
  agnes_res_env %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_internal_r_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_int") %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, clust, 
                     bpm_y_scr_external_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_external_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
                     bpm_y_scr_external_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_external_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
                     bpm_y_scr_external_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_external_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_external_r_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_ext") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, bpm_ext)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, clust, rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1, rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rsfmri_meanmotion"), names_pattern = "rsfmri_meanmotion_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "rsfmri_meanmotion")) %>%
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint)) 
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>%
  left_join(age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                 names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>%  select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% select(src_subject_id, age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
# create a new df with changeage only
changeage = age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                 names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>%
  select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>% filter(str_starts(change_timepoint, "from6mo_to"))
changeage = changeage %>% mutate(timepoint = if_else(change_timepoint == "from6mo_to1yr", "1_year_follow_up_y",
                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from1yr_to18mo", "18_month_follow_up",
                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from18mo_to2yr", "2_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from2yr_to30mo", "30_month_follow_up",
                                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from30mo_to3yr", "3_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                                     NA_character_))))))
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long, changeage)
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% mutate(changeage = if_else(!is.na(age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1) & is.na(change_timepoint), 0, changeage))

# create long version of the brain
agnes_res_env_brain_long = 
  agnes_res_env %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         dmn_dmn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_dmn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, 
         pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_dmn_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn") %>%
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, sal_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, sal)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, fpn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, fpn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "fpn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "fpn") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, fpn)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, dmn_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_sal") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, dmn_sal)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, dmn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_fpn") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, dmn_fpn)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, sal_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal_fpn") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, sal_fpn)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, clust, rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1, rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rsfmri_meanmotion"), names_pattern = "rsfmri_meanmotion_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "rsfmri_meanmotion")) %>%
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_brain_long, agnes_res_env %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, age_baseline_year_1_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
agnes_res_env_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_brain_long, age %>%
                                       pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                                    names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>%
                                       filter(timepoint=="baseline_year_1" | timepoint=="2_year_follow_up_y") %>%
                                       select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_brain_long,
                                     age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                                          names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>%
                                       filter(change_timepoint == "frombase_to2yr"), by="src_subject_id") 

# combined df
agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long = 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% 
              mutate(timepoint = case_when(
                timepoint == "6_month_follow_up" ~ "baseline_year_1", TRUE ~ timepoint)) %>% 
              select(-c(change_timepoint, changeage, rsfmri_meanmotion)), 
            agnes_res_env_brain_long %>% select(-c(age)))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, mri_info_manufacturer,
## site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, pub_base, demo_sex_v2, timepoint)`

Change in bpm across time

# baseline levels
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       124.014  62.007     2 7004.0  51.043 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  34.924  34.924     1 7677.9  28.749 8.478e-08 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base1, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7832' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7832)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7832' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7832)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.59 0.0434 Inf      1.50      1.67
##  2       2.09 0.0644 Inf      1.97      2.22
##  3       2.04 0.0599 Inf      1.93      2.16
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.5095 0.0626 Inf  -8.145  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.4588 0.0589 Inf  -7.794  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0507 0.0747 Inf   0.679  0.4972
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       141.48  70.738     2 7078.0 64.2603 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1   7.54   7.540     1 7712.2  6.8497  0.008883 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base2, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7861' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7861)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7861' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7861)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.70 0.0397 Inf      1.63      1.78
##  2       2.35 0.0602 Inf      2.23      2.47
##  3       2.02 0.0556 Inf      1.91      2.13
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.649 0.0595 Inf -10.908  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.316 0.0557 Inf  -5.668  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.333 0.0708 Inf   4.708  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# test if the change in bpm differs by groups
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         153.291 153.291     1 36601 188.883 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2)                    178.645 178.645     1 36639 220.123 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        16.960   8.480     2 44063  10.449 2.904e-05 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  26.667  26.667     1 17450  32.858 1.008e-08 ***
## changeage                    13.166  13.166     1 35450  16.223 5.643e-05 ***
## I(age^2):clust               15.306   7.653     2 41839   9.430 8.045e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
mod1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
test(emtrends(mod1, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 44580' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 44580)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 44580' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 44580)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1         0.147 0.0116 Inf  12.678  <.0001
##  2         0.219 0.0212 Inf  10.325  <.0001
##  3         0.098 0.0192 Inf   5.094  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.0718 0.0232 Inf  -3.092  0.0030
##  clust1 - clust3   0.0491 0.0215 Inf   2.281  0.0225
##  clust2 - clust3   0.1209 0.0279 Inf   4.332  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         34.877  34.877     1 35829 59.9225 1.013e-14 ***
## I(age^2)                    43.388  43.388     1 35860 74.5442 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        9.337   4.669     2 43455  8.0211 0.0003289 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  6.868   6.868     1 17407 11.7991 0.0005940 ***
## changeage                   34.789  34.789     1 34891 59.7705 1.094e-14 ***
## I(age^2):clust               3.992   1.996     2 40532  3.4291 0.0324257 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
mod2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(mod2, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 44322' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 44322)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 44322' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 44322)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend      SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1        0.0815 0.00986 Inf    0.0622     0.101
##  2        0.1249 0.01810 Inf    0.0894     0.160
##  3        0.1164 0.01640 Inf    0.0842     0.149
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.04336 0.0199 Inf  -2.184  0.0868
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.03493 0.0184 Inf  -1.896  0.0868
##  clust2 - clust3  0.00843 0.0239 Inf   0.353  0.7240
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# is the quad better fit than the linear model? had to remove weights bc of convergence
lin1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))
lin2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))
poly1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))
poly2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude))

anova(lin1, poly1)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: agnes_res_env_bpm_long
## Models:
## lin1: bpm_int ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
## poly1: bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
##       npar    AIC    BIC logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## lin1    11 178506 178601 -89242    178484                         
## poly1   12 178245 178349 -89110    178221 263.23  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lin2, poly2)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: agnes_res_env_bpm_long
## Models:
## lin2: bpm_ext ~ age * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
## poly2: bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l)
##       npar    AIC    BIC logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## lin2    11 163449 163545 -81714    163427                         
## poly2   12 163358 163462 -81667    163334 93.734  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Risk and protective factors predicting mental health

mod1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
agnes_res_env_bpm_long$resid_intmod = resid(mod1)
mod2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
agnes_res_env_bpm_long$resid_extmod = resid(mod2)
agnes_res_env_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_bpm_long, agnes_res_env[1:32])
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust)`
agnes_res_env_bpm_long[19:48] = scale(agnes_res_env_bpm_long[19:48])

agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c1 = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(clust==1)
agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c2 = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(clust==2)
agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c3 = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(clust==3)

env = colnames(agnes_res_env[3:32])

# mental health (internalizing)
mh_int_env = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_intmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c1, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_int_env) = env
mh_int_env_summ = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_int_env[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_int_env_summ) = names(mh_int_env)
mh_int_env_summ1 = lapply(mh_int_env_summ, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_int_env_summ1$clust = 1

mh_int_env2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_intmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c2, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_int_env2) = env
mh_int_env_summ2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_int_env2[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_int_env_summ2) = names(mh_int_env2)
mh_int_env_summ2 = lapply(mh_int_env_summ2, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_int_env_summ2$clust = 2

mh_int_env3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_intmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c3, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_int_env3) = env
mh_int_env_summ3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_int_env3[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_int_env_summ3) = env
mh_int_env_summ3 = lapply(mh_int_env_summ3, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_int_env_summ3$clust = 3

mh_env_summ_int = rbind(mh_int_env_summ1, mh_int_env_summ2, mh_int_env_summ3)
mh_env_summ_int$padj = p.adjust(mh_env_summ_int$`Pr(>|t|)`, method="fdr")
mh_env_summ_int$padj = round(mh_env_summ_int$padj, digits=3)
mh_env_summ_int = data.frame(mh_env_summ_int, row.names = NULL)
mh_env_summ_int
##                                          env      Estimate   Std..Error

# mental health (externalizing)
mh_env = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_extmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c1, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_env) = env
mh_env_summ = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_env[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_env_summ) = names(mh_env)
mh_env_summ1 = lapply(mh_env_summ, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_env_summ1$clust = 1

mh_env2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_extmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c2, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_env2) = env
mh_env_summ2 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_env2[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_env_summ2) = names(mh_env2)
mh_env_summ2 = lapply(mh_env_summ2, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_env_summ2$clust = 2

mh_env3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(env, "(1 | src_subject_id)"), response = "resid_extmod"),
       data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long_c3, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(mh_env3) = env
mh_env_summ3 = lapply(env, function(env) {
  model_summary = mh_env3[[env]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = env
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(mh_env_summ3) = env
mh_env_summ3 = lapply(mh_env_summ3, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "env") 
mh_env_summ3$clust = 3

mh_env_summ_ext = rbind(mh_env_summ1, mh_env_summ2, mh_env_summ3)
mh_env_summ_ext$padj = p.adjust(mh_env_summ_ext$`Pr(>|t|)`, method="fdr")
mh_env_summ_ext$padj = round(mh_env_summ_ext$padj, digits=3)
mh_env_summ_ext = data.frame(mh_env_summ_ext, row.names = NULL)
mh_env_summ_ext
##                                          env      Estimate   Std..Error
## 1                       Negative.life.events  0.0780687798 0.0083118185
## 2                             Discrimination  0.0735739598 0.0077435907
## 3                           Traumatic.events  0.0050549838 0.0097419879
## 4                                  Parenting -0.0868663110 0.0064756504
## 5                        Parental.monitoring -0.0877622041 0.0067875430
## 6                     Parent.psychopathology  0.0519994958 0.0088482828
## 7               Other.parent.psychopathology  0.0315953391 0.0095769578

Test if there is a bpm-brain association (for entire sample)

anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## dmn                       2.3224  2.3224     1 11307.3  3.6745 0.055277 . 
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 5.4137  5.4137     1  6280.7  8.5656 0.003438 **
## changeage                 0.0436  0.0436     1  3942.2  0.0690 0.792825   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.5056  1.5056     1 11219.2  2.3821 0.122761   
## mri_info_manufacturer     2.7912  1.3956     2    15.2  2.2081 0.143954   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## sal                       4.6778  4.6778     1 10964.3  7.4007 0.006530 **
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 5.4121  5.4121     1  6269.3  8.5624 0.003444 **
## changeage                 0.0642  0.0642     1  3874.0  0.1015 0.750002   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.3010  1.3010     1 11208.8  2.0582 0.151416   
## mri_info_manufacturer     2.8093  1.4046     2    14.5  2.2222 0.143839   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ fpn + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## fpn                       0.2392  0.2392     1 11237.7  0.3784 0.538488   
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 5.5238  5.5238     1  6286.1  8.7373 0.003129 **
## changeage                 0.0349  0.0349     1  3985.3  0.0552 0.814187   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         2.0653  2.0653     1 11158.6  3.2668 0.070723 . 
## mri_info_manufacturer     2.9676  1.4838     2    15.4  2.3470 0.128846   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_sal + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## dmn_sal                   3.9908  3.9908     1 11069.9  6.3131 0.011999 * 
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 5.0947  5.0947     1  6294.1  8.0594 0.004541 **
## changeage                 0.0399  0.0399     1  3931.8  0.0631 0.801697   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.0458  1.0458     1 11201.8  1.6543 0.198397   
## mri_info_manufacturer     2.9193  1.4597     2    15.1  2.3091 0.133402   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## dmn_fpn                   0.5112  0.5112     1 11300.0  0.8086 0.368567   
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 5.6002  5.6002     1  6278.5  8.8583 0.002929 **
## changeage                 0.0343  0.0343     1  3968.8  0.0542 0.815870   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         2.1380  2.1380     1 11163.3  3.3819 0.065942 . 
## mri_info_manufacturer     3.1328  1.5664     2    15.2  2.4777 0.117125   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)   
## sal_fpn                   1.2292  1.2292     1 11349.5  1.9437 0.163296   
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 5.4610  5.4610     1  6281.9  8.6356 0.003309 **
## changeage                 0.0270  0.0270     1  3955.4  0.0427 0.836342   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         2.0320  2.0320     1 11147.4  3.2133 0.073069 . 
## mri_info_manufacturer     3.2201  1.6100     2    15.0  2.5460 0.111647   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
p.adjust(c(0.692225, 0.981951, 0.632797, 0.521280, 0.012564, 0.0001441), method="fdr")
## [1] 0.8306700 0.9819510 0.8306700 0.8306700 0.0376920 0.0008646
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ dmn + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value  Pr(>F)  
## dmn                       0.43488 0.43488     1 11331.9  0.8972 0.34356  
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 0.00313 0.00313     1  6356.7  0.0065 0.93594  
## changeage                 1.07811 1.07811     1  4337.7  2.2242 0.13594  
## rsfmri_meanmotion         1.74162 1.74162     1 11117.4  3.5930 0.05805 .
## mri_info_manufacturer     1.95652 0.97826     2    19.5  2.0182 0.15967  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ sal + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))

Test if bpm-brain association differs by groups

anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value   Pr(>F)

## clust                     6.8821  3.4410     2 10880.1  5.4955 0.004116 **
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 5.5249  5.5249     1  5912.0  8.8236 0.002986 **
## changeage                 0.0867  0.0867     1  2558.2  0.1385 0.709851   
## rsfmri_meanmotion         0.9590  0.9590     1 11054.5  1.5315 0.215911

## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    0.209   0.209     1 11300.7  0.4373 0.5084    
## clust                     67.692  33.846     2  5163.0 70.7445 <2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  0.009   0.009     1  6150.7  0.0187 0.8911    
## changeage                  0.937   0.937     1  3270.6  1.9581 0.1618    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          1.210   1.210     1 10985.8  2.5283 0.1118    
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.302   0.651     2    10.8  1.3607 0.2971    
## sal_fpn:clust              1.044   0.522     2 11339.7  1.0910 0.3359    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
p.adjust(c(0.989393, 0.76681, 0.5743272, 0.9845, 0.5773, 0.2175), method="fdr")
## [1] 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393 0.989393
networks = c( "dmn_fpn", "sal_fpn")

moddmnfpn = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
modsalfpn = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))

test(emtrends(moddmnfpn, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11413' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11413)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11413' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11413)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             1.211 0.564 Inf   2.147  0.0318
##  2            -1.788 1.100 Inf  -1.623  0.1047
##  3            -0.767 1.050 Inf  -0.729  0.4658
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2     3.00 1.24 Inf   2.427  0.0456
##  clust1 - clust3     1.98 1.19 Inf   1.662  0.1447
##  clust2 - clust3    -1.02 1.52 Inf  -0.671  0.5021
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
test(emtrends(modsalfpn, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11409' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11409)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11409' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11409)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.707 0.568 Inf   1.246  0.2127
##  2             4.163 1.120 Inf   3.732  0.0002
##  3            -2.456 1.040 Inf  -2.358  0.0184
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -3.46 1.25 Inf  -2.771  0.0075
##  clust1 - clust3     3.16 1.18 Inf   2.673  0.0075
##  clust2 - clust3     6.62 1.52 Inf   4.346  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests

Exploratory differences by dev timing

anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                    1.3998  1.3998     1  9991.7  2.2401   0.13450    
## clust                      7.5334  3.7667     2  9922.7  6.0278   0.00242 ** 
## age                        0.8858  0.8858     1  9119.1  1.4175   0.23385    
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 11.5076 11.5076     1  7851.5 18.4155 1.797e-05 ***
## changeage                  0.1618  0.1618     1  2612.5  0.2589   0.61089    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          3.2195  3.2195     1 10951.0  5.1521   0.02324 *  
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.9740  0.9870     2     7.3  1.5795   0.26915    
## dmn_fpn:clust              4.1929  2.0964     2  9977.0  3.3549   0.03495 *  
## dmn_fpn:age                1.2855  1.2855     1  9892.3  2.0572   0.15152    
## clust:age                  5.4486  2.7243     2  9812.8  4.3596   0.01281 *  
## dmn_fpn:clust:age          3.6563  1.8282     2  9879.9  2.9256   0.05368 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                            Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    0.2546  0.2546     1 10081.1  0.4080 0.5230140    
## clust                      4.7553  2.3777     2  9672.1  3.8093 0.0221967 *  
## age                        8.0638  8.0638     1  8141.6 12.9192 0.0003272 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 11.4541 11.4541     1  7833.0 18.3509 1.859e-05 ***
## changeage                  0.1818  0.1818     1  2557.1  0.2913 0.5894245    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          2.8301  2.8301     1 10938.2  4.5342 0.0332471 *  
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.9732  0.9866     2     7.1  1.5806 0.2709181    
## sal_fpn:clust              4.2549  2.1275     2 10090.8  3.4085 0.0331297 *  
## sal_fpn:age                0.4035  0.4035     1 10082.0  0.6465 0.4213759    
## clust:age                  5.1024  2.5512     2  9567.0  4.0873 0.0168133 *  
## sal_fpn:clust:age          5.7808  2.8904     2 10081.5  4.6308 0.0097676 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
moddmnfpn_age = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
modsalfpn_age = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust * age + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))

agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% dplyr::summarize(mean = mean(age, na.rm=T), sd = sd(age, na.rm=T))
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##    mean    sd
##   <dbl> <dbl>
## 1  11.7  1.07
test(emtrends(moddmnfpn_age, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11413' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11413)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11413' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11413)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
## $emtrends
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             1.245 0.566 Inf   2.199  0.0279
##  2            -1.622 1.110 Inf  -1.461  0.1441
##  3            -0.943 1.050 Inf  -0.896  0.3703
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    2.867 1.24 Inf   2.304  0.0636
##  clust1 - clust3    2.188 1.19 Inf   1.836  0.0996
##  clust2 - clust3   -0.679 1.53 Inf  -0.444  0.6570
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
test(emtrends(modsalfpn_age, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11409' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11409)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11409' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11409)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.778 0.569 Inf   1.366  0.1720
##  2             4.156 1.120 Inf   3.727  0.0002
##  3            -2.412 1.040 Inf  -2.311  0.0208
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -3.38 1.25 Inf  -2.708  0.0072
##  clust1 - clust3     3.19 1.19 Inf   2.690  0.0072
##  clust2 - clust3     6.57 1.52 Inf   4.310  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
networks = c("dmn_fpn", "sal_fpn")

bpmbrain_int_age = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(paste0(net, "*clust*age"), "age_baseline_year_1_arm_1", "changeage", 
                                  "(1 | src_subject_id)", "(1 | site_id_l)",
                                  "rsfmri_meanmotion"), response = "bpm_int"),
       data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(bpmbrain_int_age) = networks

bpmbrain_int_age_summ1 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  model_summary = bpmbrain_int_age[[net]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = paste0(net, ":clust2:age")
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(bpmbrain_int_age_summ1) = names(bpmbrain_int_age)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ1 = lapply(bpmbrain_int_age_summ1, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "network") 

bpmbrain_int_age_summ2 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  model_summary = bpmbrain_int_age[[net]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = paste0(net, ":clust3:age")
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(bpmbrain_int_age_summ2) = names(bpmbrain_int_age)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ2 = lapply(bpmbrain_int_age_summ2, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "network") 

bpmbrain_int_age2 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  lmer(reformulate(termlabels = c(paste0(net, "*relevel(clust,ref=2)*age"), "age_baseline_year_1_arm_1", "changeage", 
                                  "(1 | src_subject_id)", "(1 | site_id_l)", "mri_info_manufacturer",
                                  "rsfmri_meanmotion"), response = "bpm_int"),
       data = agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights) |>
    summary()})
names(bpmbrain_int_age2) = networks
bpmbrain_int_age_summ3 = lapply(networks, function(net) {
  model_summary = bpmbrain_int_age2[[net]]
  coeffs = model_summary$coefficients
  row_name = paste0(net, ":relevel(clust, ref = 2)3:age")
  int_row = coeffs[rownames(coeffs) == row_name, , drop = FALSE]})
names(bpmbrain_int_age_summ3) = names(bpmbrain_int_age2)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ3 = lapply(bpmbrain_int_age_summ3, function(coeffs) {
  coeffs_df = as.data.frame(coeffs)
  coeffs_df$term = rownames(coeffs_df) 
  coeffs_df}) %>% bind_rows(.id = "network") 

bpmbrain_int_age_summ = rbind(bpmbrain_int_age_summ1, bpmbrain_int_age_summ2, bpmbrain_int_age_summ3)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ$padj = p.adjust(bpmbrain_int_age_summ$`Pr(>|t|)`, method="fdr")
bpmbrain_int_age_summ = data.frame(bpmbrain_int_age_summ, row.names = NULL)
bpmbrain_int_age_summ
##   network   Estimate Std..Error        df    t.value    Pr...t..
## 1 dmn_fpn  0.6307094   1.062839  9955.275  0.5934195 0.552913938
## 2 sal_fpn  2.8814470   1.098473 10186.702  2.6231394 0.008725341
## 3 dmn_fpn  2.6205330   1.035809  9859.637  2.5299387 0.011423686
## 4 sal_fpn -0.9422866   1.044756 10004.389 -0.9019199 0.367121106
## 5 dmn_fpn  1.8811813   1.313894  9893.809  1.4317608 0.152243881
## 6 sal_fpn -3.8668429   1.339543 10095.518 -2.8866885 0.003901454
##                                   term       padj
## 1                   dmn_fpn:clust2:age 0.55291394
## 2                   sal_fpn:clust2:age 0.02284737
## 3                   dmn_fpn:clust3:age 0.02284737
## 4                   sal_fpn:clust3:age 0.44054533
## 5 dmn_fpn:relevel(clust, ref = 2)3:age 0.22836582
## 6 sal_fpn:relevel(clust, ref = 2)3:age 0.02284737

Sensitivity analysis with puberty and sex

# baseline mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       103.080  51.540     2 6981.5 42.7649 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  41.881  41.881     1 7480.1 34.7502 3.911e-09 ***
## pub_base                     22.283  22.283     1 7616.4 18.4892 1.730e-05 ***
## sex                           9.647   9.647     1 7646.5  8.0049  0.004677 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp1, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7653' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7653)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7653' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7653)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.58 0.0433 Inf      1.50      1.67
##  2       2.08 0.0646 Inf      1.95      2.20
##  3       1.99 0.0611 Inf      1.87      2.11
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.4937 0.0630 Inf  -7.830  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.4072 0.0605 Inf  -6.730  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0864 0.0756 Inf   1.143  0.2531
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       130.810  65.405     2 7013.6 60.0822 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  11.484  11.484     1 7496.6 10.5494  0.001167 ** 
## pub_base                      5.762   5.762     1 7645.3  5.2927  0.021442 *  
## sex                          60.364  60.364     1 7671.5 55.4517 1.062e-13 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp2, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 7679' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 7679)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 7679' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 7679)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.70 0.0397 Inf      1.62      1.78
##  2       2.34 0.0605 Inf      2.22      2.46
##  3       2.00 0.0568 Inf      1.88      2.11
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.639 0.0599 Inf -10.663  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.294 0.0571 Inf  -5.141  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.345 0.0716 Inf   4.815  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# change over time mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF  F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         148.276 148.276     1 35922 184.9102 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2)                    173.771 173.771     1 35958 216.7042 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        12.102   6.051     2 43157   7.5462 0.0005288 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  36.163  36.163     1 16527  45.0983 1.935e-11 ***
## changeage                    12.172  12.172     1 34791  15.1796 9.794e-05 ***
## pub_base                     44.249  44.249     1 12619  55.1820 1.169e-13 ***
## sex                          62.357  62.357     1 12978  77.7630 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2):clust               16.146   8.073     2 41025  10.0674 4.254e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp1 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp1, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.

## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43581' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43581)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.

##  2         0.228 0.0213 Inf    0.1863     0.270
##  3         0.103 0.0196 Inf    0.0642     0.141
##

## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic

##  clust2 - clust3   0.1254 0.0282 Inf   4.451  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         33.165  33.165     1 35030 57.5164 3.435e-14 ***
## I(age^2)                    41.306  41.306     1 35059 71.6334 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        7.966   3.983     2 42516  6.9072 0.0010017 ** 
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 10.320  10.320     1 16782 17.8975 2.344e-05 ***
## changeage                   33.841  33.841     1 34111 58.6880 1.896e-14 ***
## pub_base                     8.633   8.633     1 13380 14.9713 0.0001097 ***
