## I(age^2):clust               4.117   2.059     2 39612  3.5703 0.0281553 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp2 = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l) + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
test(emtrends(supp2, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 43331' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 43331)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend      SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        0.0805 0.00993 Inf   8.111  <.0001
##  2        0.1279 0.01820 Inf   7.041  <.0001
##  3        0.1128 0.01670 Inf   6.739  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.0474 0.0199 Inf  -2.374  0.0528
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.0323 0.0187 Inf  -1.723  0.1272
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0151 0.0241 Inf   0.625  0.5317
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# brain-beh among clusters #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    1.145  1.1454     1 11003.3  1.8540  0.173347    
## clust                     60.022 30.0110     2  4600.4 48.5789 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  8.211  8.2112     1  5640.9 13.2916  0.000269 ***
## changeage                  0.083  0.0832     1  2366.0  0.1346  0.713701    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          2.218  2.2181     1 10596.8  3.5904  0.058140 .  
## mri_info_manufacturer      2.151  1.0756     2     6.9  1.7411  0.244437    
## pub_base                  16.116 16.1160     1  5743.3 26.0870 3.369e-07 ***
## sex                        2.340  2.3404     1  6143.1  3.7884  0.051655 .  
## sal_fpn:clust             12.277  6.1383     2 11061.4  9.9361 4.883e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp3 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp3, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11151' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11151)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1             0.685 0.569 Inf    -0.429     1.799
##  2             4.212 1.120 Inf     2.008     6.415
##  3            -2.646 1.060 Inf    -4.715    -0.577
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -3.53 1.25 Inf  -2.810  0.0053
##  clust1 - clust3     3.33 1.20 Inf   2.786  0.0053
##  clust2 - clust3     6.86 1.54 Inf   4.456  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF   DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                    0.428  0.4283     1 10960.3  0.6927 0.4052813    
## clust                     48.403 24.2015     2  7807.6 39.1390 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  8.401  8.4013     1  5664.5 13.5866 0.0002299 ***
## changeage                  0.061  0.0613     1  2437.4  0.0992 0.7528094    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          2.520  2.5202     1 10626.1  4.0757 0.0435304 *  
## mri_info_manufacturer      2.158  1.0788     2     7.2  1.7446 0.2409887    
## pub_base                  16.286 16.2858     1  5759.7 26.3377  2.96e-07 ***
## sex                        2.298  2.2985     1  6142.8  3.7171 0.0539038 .  
## dmn_fpn:clust              3.907  1.9536     2 11050.6  3.1594 0.0424877 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp4 = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer + pub_base + sex, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp4, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11155' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11155)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11155' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11155)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1             1.071 0.566 Inf   -0.0388     2.181
##  2            -1.820 1.100 Inf   -3.9782     0.339
##  3            -0.624 1.080 Inf   -2.7406     1.492
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2     2.89 1.24 Inf   2.339  0.0580
##  clust1 - clust3     1.70 1.22 Inf   1.394  0.2449
##  clust2 - clust3    -1.20 1.54 Inf  -0.776  0.4377
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer, sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests

Sensitivity analyses with random sampling of sib

Create dfs

set.seed(123) 
agnes_res_env_sample = agnes_res_env %>%
  group_by(rel_family_id) %>% 
  slice_sample(n = 1) %>% 
  ungroup() 

# create long version of bpm

# bpm long (6 timepoints)
# create long version of bpm
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = 
  agnes_res_env_sample %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
         bpm_y_scr_internal_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_internal_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_internal_r_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_int") %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, clust, 
                     bpm_y_scr_external_r_6_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_external_r_1_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
                     bpm_y_scr_external_r_18_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_external_r_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1,
                     bpm_y_scr_external_r_30_month_follow_up_arm_1, bpm_y_scr_external_r_3_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("bpm_y_scr"), names_pattern = "bpm_y_scr_external_r_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "bpm_ext") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, bpm_ext)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, clust, rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1, rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rsfmri_meanmotion"), names_pattern = "rsfmri_meanmotion_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "rsfmri_meanmotion")) %>%
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint)) 
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>%
  left_join(age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                 names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>%  select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% select(src_subject_id, age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
# create a new df with changeage only
changeage = age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                 names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>%
  select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>% filter(str_starts(change_timepoint, "from6mo_to"))
changeage = changeage %>% mutate(timepoint = if_else(change_timepoint == "from6mo_to1yr", "1_year_follow_up_y",
                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from1yr_to18mo", "18_month_follow_up",
                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from18mo_to2yr", "2_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                     if_else(change_timepoint == "from2yr_to30mo", "30_month_follow_up",
                                                                             if_else(change_timepoint == "from30mo_to3yr", "3_year_follow_up_y",
                                                                                     NA_character_))))))
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, changeage)
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% 
  mutate(changeage = if_else(!is.na(age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1) & is.na(change_timepoint), 0, changeage))

# create long version of the brain
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = 
  agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
  select(src_subject_id, clust, 
         dmn_dmn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_dmn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1, 
         mri_info_manufacturer, site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, 
         pub_base, demo_sex_v2) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_dmn_(.*)_arm_1",
               names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn") %>%
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, sal_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, sal)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, fpn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, fpn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "fpn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "fpn") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, fpn)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, dmn_sal_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_sal_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_sal_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_sal") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, dmn_sal)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, dmn_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, dmn_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "dmn_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "dmn_fpn") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, dmn_fpn)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, sal_fpn_baseline_year_1_arm_1, sal_fpn_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = ends_with("arm_1"), names_pattern = "sal_fpn_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "sal_fpn") %>% select(src_subject_id, timepoint, sal_fpn)) %>% 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
              select(src_subject_id, clust, rsfmri_meanmotion_baseline_year_1_arm_1, rsfmri_meanmotion_2_year_follow_up_y_arm_1) %>%
              pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rsfmri_meanmotion"), names_pattern = "rsfmri_meanmotion_(.*)_arm_1",
                           names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "rsfmri_meanmotion")) %>%
  mutate(timepoint = as.factor(timepoint))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long, agnes_res_env_sample  %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, age_baseline_year_1_arm_1))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id)`
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long, age %>%
                                       pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("age_"), names_pattern = "age_(.*)_arm_1",
                                                    names_to = "timepoint", values_to = "age") %>%
                                       filter(timepoint=="baseline_year_1" | timepoint=="2_year_follow_up_y") %>%
                                       select(src_subject_id, timepoint, age))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, timepoint)`
agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long = left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long,
                                     age %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("changeage_"), names_pattern = "changeage_(.*)",
                                                          names_to = "change_timepoint", values_to = "changeage") %>% 
                                       select(src_subject_id, change_timepoint, changeage) %>%
                                       filter(change_timepoint == "frombase_to2yr"), by="src_subject_id") 

# combined df
agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long = 
  left_join(agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% 
              mutate(timepoint = case_when(
                timepoint == "6_month_follow_up" ~ "baseline_year_1", TRUE ~ timepoint)) %>% 
              select(-c(change_timepoint, changeage, rsfmri_meanmotion)), 
            agnes_res_env_sample_brain_long %>% select(-c(age)))
## Joining with `by = join_by(src_subject_id, clust, mri_info_manufacturer,
## site_id_l, rel_family_id, sex, weights, pub_base, demo_sex_v2, timepoint)`

Test models using randomly sampled datasets

# baseline mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       120.835  60.418     2 6095.3   49.18 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  30.087  30.087     1 6585.9   24.49 7.654e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp1_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp1_samp, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 6642' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 6642)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 6642' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 6642)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.58 0.0464 Inf      1.49      1.67
##  2       2.14 0.0694 Inf      2.00      2.27
##  3       2.05 0.0632 Inf      1.93      2.17
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.5573 0.0681 Inf  -8.183  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3  -0.4697 0.0627 Inf  -7.489  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0876 0.0801 Inf   1.094  0.2741
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## clust                       137.273  68.636     2 6105.4 61.7503 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1   8.333   8.333     1 6609.1  7.4965  0.006198 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
base_supp2_samp = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long %>% filter(timepoint == "6_month_follow_up"), weights = weights))
emmeans(base_supp2_samp, pairwise ~ clust, adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 6667' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 6667)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 6667' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 6667)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emmeans
##  clust emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1       1.69 0.0430 Inf      1.60      1.77
##  2       2.37 0.0653 Inf      2.24      2.50
##  3       2.04 0.0591 Inf      1.93      2.16
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2   -0.684 0.0648 Inf -10.563  <.0001
##  clust1 - clust3   -0.357 0.0593 Inf  -6.015  <.0001
##  clust2 - clust3    0.327 0.0760 Inf   4.310  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# change over time mental health #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                              Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF  F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         128.334 128.334     1 30989 155.0816 < 2.2e-16 ***
## I(age^2)                    149.065 149.065     1 31020 180.1322 < 2.2e-16 ***
## clust                        17.846   8.923     2 37290  10.7826 2.082e-05 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  16.478  16.478     1 14460  19.9126 8.168e-06 ***
## changeage                    11.252  11.252     1 30026  13.5969  0.000227 ***
## I(age^2):clust                6.434   3.217     2 35396   3.8877  0.020502 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp1_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp1_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 37730' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 37730)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 37730' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 37730)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1         0.152 0.0128 Inf    0.1267     0.177
##  2         0.188 0.0231 Inf    0.1426     0.233
##  3         0.106 0.0204 Inf    0.0656     0.146
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2  -0.0362 0.0254 Inf  -1.423  0.1548
##  clust1 - clust3   0.0461 0.0231 Inf   2.001  0.0681
##  clust2 - clust3   0.0823 0.0301 Inf   2.736  0.0186
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                             Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## age                         31.465  31.465     1 30344 53.6258 2.486e-13 ***
## I(age^2)                    37.882  37.882     1 30370 64.5616 9.693e-16 ***
## clust                       15.881   7.941     2 36788 13.5331 1.333e-06 ***
## age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1  3.600   3.600     1 14653  6.1358   0.01326 *  
## changeage                   26.615  26.615     1 29566 45.3590 1.670e-11 ***
## I(age^2):clust               0.738   0.369     2 34270  0.6290   0.53314    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp2_samp = (lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), agnes_res_env_sample_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
test(emtrends(supp2_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "age", adjust="fdr"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 37535' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 37535)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 37535' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 37535)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust age.trend     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1        0.0812 0.0108 Inf   7.510  <.0001
##  2        0.0948 0.0196 Inf   4.829  <.0001
##  3        0.1017 0.0173 Inf   5.869  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2 -0.01360 0.0216 Inf  -0.629  0.7847
##  clust1 - clust3 -0.02058 0.0196 Inf  -1.049  0.7847
##  clust2 - clust3 -0.00698 0.0256 Inf  -0.273  0.7847
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
# brain-beh among clusters #
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## sal_fpn                    1.161   1.161     1 9572.9  1.8104  0.178494    
## clust                     67.550  33.775     2 4178.4 52.6760 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  4.813   4.813     1 5056.2  7.5058  0.006172 ** 
## changeage                  0.101   0.101     1 2449.0  0.1581  0.690988    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          0.447   0.447     1 9314.5  0.6965  0.403980    
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.506   0.753     2    8.7  1.1742  0.353726    
## sal_fpn:clust             13.033   6.516     2 9608.0 10.1633 3.898e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp3_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp3_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "sal_fpn", adjust="fdr")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 9642' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 9642)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 9642' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 9642)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## $emtrends
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  1             0.571 0.635 Inf    -0.672     1.815
##  2             4.634 1.230 Inf     2.230     7.039
##  3            -2.809 1.110 Inf    -4.984    -0.633
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  clust1 - clust2    -4.06 1.38 Inf  -2.952  0.0047
##  clust1 - clust3     3.38 1.28 Inf   2.650  0.0080
##  clust2 - clust3     7.44 1.65 Inf   4.508  <.0001
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: fdr method for 3 tests
anova(lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
## Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
##                           Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF  DenDF F value    Pr(>F)    
## dmn_fpn                    0.218  0.2177     1 9532.1  0.3392  0.560321    
## clust                     51.892 25.9458     2 6858.7 40.4212 < 2.2e-16 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1  4.987  4.9868     1 5061.3  7.7691  0.005335 ** 
## changeage                  0.077  0.0769     1 2500.7  0.1198  0.729255    
## rsfmri_meanmotion          0.540  0.5404     1 9331.8  0.8419  0.358875    
## mri_info_manufacturer      1.558  0.7789     2    9.0  1.2134  0.341483    
## dmn_fpn:clust              3.538  1.7691     2 9573.7  2.7562  0.063586 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
supp4_samp = (lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_sample_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights))
emtrends(supp4_samp, pairwise ~ clust, var = "dmn_fpn", adjust="fdr")

Plot/Visualize results

Demographic table

agnes_res_env %>% group_by(clust) %>% dplyr::summarize(median(demo_comb_income_v2, na.rm = T))
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   clust `median(demo_comb_income_v2, na.rm = T)`
##   <fct>                                    <int>
## 1 1                                            9
## 2 2                                            8
## 3 3                                            6
agnes_res_env = agnes_res_env %>% 
  mutate(demo_sex_v2 = as.factor(demo_sex_v2), 
         race_ethnicity = as.factor(race_ethnicity), 
         demo_comb_income_v2 = as.factor(demo_comb_income_v2))
label(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2) = "Gender"
label(agnes_res_env$age_baseline_year_1_arm_1) = "Age"
label(agnes_res_env$race_ethnicity) = "Race/Ethnicity"
label(agnes_res_env$demo_comb_income_v2) = "Household Income"
label(agnes_res_env$demo_prnt_ed_v2) = "Parent Education"
agnes_res_env$clust_num = as.factor(as.numeric(agnes_res_env$clust))
levels(agnes_res_env$clust_num) = c("Low risk (66%)", "Trauma, parental and\nfamily risk (16%)", "Disadvantage 18%)")

table1(~ demo_sex_v2 + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + race_ethnicity + 
         demo_comb_income_v2 + demo_prnt_ed_v2 | clust_num, 
       data=agnes_res_env)

# compare demo
summary(aov(age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 ~ clust, agnes_res_env))
##               Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)
## clust          2    1.7  0.8748   2.237  0.107
## Residuals   8075 3158.2  0.3911
summary(aov(demo_prnt_ed_v2 ~ clust, agnes_res_env))
##               Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)    
## clust          2   6700    3350   627.9 <2e-16 ***
## Residuals   8065  43033       5                   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 10 observations deleted due to missingness
chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2, agnes_res_env$clust))
## Warning in chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2, agnes_res_env$clust)):
## Chi-squared approximation may be incorrect
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  table(agnes_res_env$demo_sex_v2, agnes_res_env$clust)
## X-squared = 7.0466, df = 4, p-value = 0.1334
chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$race_ethnicity, agnes_res_env$clust))
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  table(agnes_res_env$race_ethnicity, agnes_res_env$clust)
## X-squared = 1481.5, df = 8, p-value < 2.2e-16
chisq.test(table(agnes_res_env$demo_comb_income_v2, agnes_res_env$clust))
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  table(agnes_res_env$demo_comb_income_v2, agnes_res_env$clust)
## X-squared = 1580.9, df = 18, p-value < 2.2e-16
agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% group_by(timepoint) %>% dplyr::summarize(mean(age, na.rm=T), sd(age, na.rm=T))
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   timepoint          `mean(age, na.rm = T)` `sd(age, na.rm = T)`
##   <chr>                               <dbl>                <dbl>
## 1 18_month_follow_up                   11.4                0.636
## 2 1_year_follow_up_y                   10.9                0.642
## 3 2_year_follow_up_y                   12.0                0.661
## 4 30_month_follow_up                   12.4                0.639
## 5 3_year_follow_up_y                   12.9                0.642
## 6 6_month_follow_up                    10.4                0.631

Comparison of environmental factors

### pairwise comparison
# standardize all first
agnes_res_env_std = agnes_res_env %>% mutate(across(3:32, ~ scale(.x, center=F)))
var = colnames(agnes_res_env_std[3:32])

pairwise = function(var) {
  formula = as.formula(paste0("`", var, "` ~ clust"))
  df = agnes_res_env_std[c("clust", var)]
  fit = aov(formula, data = df)
  
  emms = emmeans(fit, "clust")
  pairwise_contrasts = summary(
    contrast(emms, method = "pairwise", adjust = "none"),
    infer = c(TRUE, TRUE)  # ensure CIs and p-values
  ) %>% as.data.frame()
  
  # Rename CI columns if present
  names(pairwise_contrasts)[names(pairwise_contrasts) %in% c("lower.CL", "asymp.LCL")] <- "CI_lower"
  names(pairwise_contrasts)[names(pairwise_contrasts) %in% c("upper.CL", "asymp.UCL")] <- "CI_upper"
  
  # Ensure CI columns exist before selecting
  if (!all(c("CI_lower", "CI_upper") %in% names(pairwise_contrasts))) {
    stop("Confidence intervals not present in summary output.")
  }

  comp = pairwise_contrasts %>%
    rename(
      comparison = contrast,
      mean_diff = estimate
    ) %>%
    mutate(var = var) %>%
    select(var, comparison, mean_diff, SE, CI_lower, CI_upper, p.value)
  
  d_df = eff_size(emms, sigma = sigma(fit), edf = df.residual(fit)) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rename(comparison = contrast, cohen_d = effect.size) %>%
    select(comparison, cohen_d)
  
  comp = left_join(comp, d_df, by = "comparison")
  return(comp)
}

pairwise_results = map_dfr(var, pairwise)

pairwise_results %>%
  mutate(pfdr = p.adjust(p.value, method = "fdr")) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%
  kable("html") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
var comparison mean_diff SE CI_lower CI_upper p.value cohen_d pfdr

Urbanicity clust1 - clust2 0.179 0.014 0.152 0.206 0.000 0.407 0.000
Urbanicity clust1 - clust3 0.209 0.013 0.184 0.235 0.000 0.476 0.000
Urbanicity clust2 - clust3 0.030 0.017 -0.003 0.063 0.070 0.069 0.076
Lead.risk clust1 - clust2 0.020 0.014 -0.008 0.048 0.171 0.043 0.177
Lead.risk clust1 - clust3 -0.710 0.014 -0.736 -0.683 0.000 -1.539 0.000
Lead.risk clust2 - clust3 -0.729 0.018 -0.764 -0.695 0.000 -1.582 0.000
Air.pollution..PM.2.5. clust1 - clust2 -0.044 0.006 -0.056 -0.032 0.000 -0.216 0.000
Air.pollution..PM.2.5. clust1 - clust3 -0.164 0.006 -0.176 -0.153 0.000 -0.806 0.000
Air.pollution..PM.2.5. clust2 - clust3 -0.120 0.008 -0.136 -0.105 0.000 -0.590 0.000
Area.deprivation.index clust1 - clust2 -0.052 0.007 -0.065 -0.039 0.000 -0.237 0.000
Area.deprivation.index clust1 - clust3 -0.218 0.006 -0.231 -0.205 0.000 -0.993 0.000
Area.deprivation.index clust2 - clust3 -0.166 0.008 -0.182 -0.150 0.000 -0.756 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..education. clust1 - clust2 0.097 0.010 0.077 0.117 0.000 0.296 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..education. clust1 - clust3 0.642 0.010 0.623 0.661 0.000 1.958 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..education. clust2 - clust3 0.545 0.013 0.520 0.569 0.000 1.662 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..health. clust1 - clust2 0.095 0.011 0.074 0.115 0.000 0.280 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..health. clust1 - clust3 0.664 0.010 0.645 0.684 0.000 1.962 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..health. clust2 - clust3 0.569 0.013 0.544 0.595 0.000 1.682 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..social.economic. clust1 - clust2 0.073 0.010 0.054 0.092 0.000 0.236 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..social.economic. clust1 - clust3 0.710 0.009 0.692 0.728 0.000 2.281 0.000
Child.opportunity.index..social.economic. clust2 - clust3 0.637 0.012 0.614 0.660 0.000 2.046 0.000

Mental health trajectories

# mental health outcomes
custom_colors = c("1" = "grey", "2" = "#F3D8C7", "3" = "#7C5B54") 

# fit models
mod1 = lmer(bpm_int ~ age + I(age^2)  * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l), data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights)
mod2 = lmer(bpm_ext ~ age + I(age^2) * clust + age_6_month_follow_up_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + (1 | site_id_l),
            data = agnes_res_env_bpm_long, na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights)

# get predicted scores
preds = ggpredict(mod1, terms = c("age [all]", "clust"))
preds2 = ggpredict(mod2, terms = c("age [all]", "clust"))

# plot predicted lines for each cluster
int = plot(preds, line_size=1, show_title=FALSE) + labs(x = "Age", y = "Predicted Internalizing Score") +
  theme_minimal() + theme_classic() + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(legend.position="none") + geom_rug(alpha = 0.3, position = position_jitter(width = 0, height = 0))
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
ext = plot(preds2, line_size=1, show_title=FALSE) + labs(x = "Age", y = "Predicted Externalizing Score") +
  theme_minimal() + theme_classic() + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(legend.position="none") + geom_rug(alpha = 0.3, position = position_jitter(width = 0, height = 0))
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
# combine
ggarrange(int, ext, nrow=1)

# quad plot by sex
agnes_res_env_bpm_long$clust = as.factor(agnes_res_env_bpm_long$clust)

int_sex = ggplot(agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)) %>% filter(!is.na(sex)) %>% mutate(sex = case_when(sex == 1 ~ "Male", sex == 2 ~ "Female", TRUE ~ NA_character_)), 
       aes(x = age, y = bpm_int, color = factor(clust), linetype = factor(clust), fill=clust)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x + I(x^2), se = TRUE) + 
  geom_rug(sides = "b", alpha = 0.3, position = position_jitter(width = 0, height = 0)) +
  theme_classic() + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Internalizing Symptoms") + scale_linetype_manual(values = c("dashed", "solid", "solid")) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1.25, 3.25)) + facet_grid(~sex) + theme(legend.position="none")
ext_sex = ggplot(agnes_res_env_bpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)) %>% filter(!is.na(sex)) %>% mutate(sex = case_when(sex == 1 ~ "Male", sex == 2 ~ "Female", TRUE ~ NA_character_)), 
       aes(x = age, y = bpm_ext, color = factor(clust), linetype = factor(clust), fill=clust)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x + I(x^2), se = TRUE) + 
  geom_rug(sides = "b", alpha = 0.3, position = position_jitter(width = 0, height = 0)) +
  theme_classic() + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Externalizing Symptoms") + scale_linetype_manual(values = c("dashed", "solid", "solid")) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1.25, 3.25)) + facet_grid(~sex) + theme(legend.position="none")
ggarrange(int_sex, ext_sex, nrow=2, ncol=1)
## Warning: Removed 1370 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 1628 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).

Risk and protective factors predicting bpm

# plot
protective_env_int = mh_env_summ_int %>% filter(Estimate < 0 & padj < 0.05)
protective_env_ext = mh_env_summ_ext %>% filter(Estimate < 0 & padj < 0.05)
protective_env_int$env_label = dplyr::recode(protective_env_int$env,
                                             "fes_org" = "Family\norganization", "fes_cohesion" = "Family\ncohesion",
                                             "familism" = "Familism", "comc" = "Community\ncohesion", "peers_prosocial" = "Prosocial\npeers",
                                             "srpf" = "Protective\nschool", "crpbi" = "Parental\nacceptance", "coi_ed" = "COI\n(education)",
                                             "coi_se" = "COI\n(soc/econ)",  "nsc" = "Neighborhood\nsafety", "pm" = "Parental\nmonitoring")
protective_env_ext$env_label = dplyr::recode(protective_env_ext$env,
                                             "fes_org" = "Family\norganization", "fes_cohesion" = "Family\ncohesion",
                                             "familism" = "Familism", "comc" = "Community\ncohesion", "peers_prosocial" = "Prosocial\npeers",
                                             "srpf" = "Protective\nschool", "crpbi" = "Parental\nacceptance", "coi_ed" = "COI\n(education)", 
                                             "coi_se" = "COI\n(soc/econ)",  "nsc" = "Neighborhood\nsafety", "pm" = "Parental\nmonitoring")
protective_env_int = protective_env_int %>%
  mutate(env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
         env_label_faceted = fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate, .desc=T))
resilience_int = ggplot(protective_env_int, aes(x = env_label_faceted, y = Estimate)) + geom_point(color = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * Std..Error, ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * Std..Error),
                width = 0.2, color = "steelblue") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("_.*", "", x)) +
  facet_wrap(~ clust, scales = "free") + coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Environmental Variable", y = "Effect on Residual Internalizing Symptoms") + theme_classic()
protective_env_ext = protective_env_ext %>%
  mutate(env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
         env_label_faceted = fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate, .desc=T))
resilience_ext = ggplot(protective_env_ext, aes(x = env_label_faceted, y = Estimate)) + geom_point(color = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * Std..Error, ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * Std..Error),
                width = 0.2, color = "steelblue") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("_.*", "", x)) +
  facet_wrap(~ clust, scales = "free") + coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Environmental Variable", y = "Effect on Residual Externalizing Symptoms") + theme_classic()

risk_env_int = mh_env_summ_int %>% filter(Estimate > 0 & padj < 0.05)
risk_env_ext = mh_env_summ_ext %>% filter(Estimate > 0 & padj < 0.05)
risk_env_int$env_label = dplyr::recode(risk_env_int$env,
                                       "dm" = "Discrimination", "peers_rulebreak" = "Rule-breaking\npeers",
                                       "peq" = "Peer aggression\nvictim", "fes_conflict" = "Family\nconflict",
                                       "lec_bad" = "Negative\nlife events", "asr" = "Parent\npsychopathology",
                                       "abcl" = "Second parent\npsychopathology", "nbhsoc" = "Neighborhood\ndisadv.",
                                       "adi" = "ADI")
risk_env_ext$env_label = dplyr::recode(risk_env_ext$env,
                                       "dm" = "Discrimination", "peers_rulebreak" = "Rule-breaking\npeers",
                                       "peq" = "Peer aggression\nvictim", "fes_conflict" = "Family\nconflict",
                                       "lec_bad" = "Negative\nlife events", "asr" = "Parent\npsychopathology",
                                       "abcl" = "Second parent\npsychopathology", "nbhsoc" = "Neighborhood\ndisadv.",
                                       "adi" = "ADI")
risk_env_int = risk_env_int %>%
  mutate(env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
         env_label_faceted = fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate))
risk_int = ggplot(risk_env_int, aes(x = env_label_faceted, y = Estimate)) + geom_point(color = "darkred") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * Std..Error, ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * Std..Error),
                width = 0.2, color = "darkred") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("_.*", "", x)) +
  facet_wrap(~ clust, scales = "free") + coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Environmental Variable", y = "Effect on Residual Internalizing Symptoms") + theme_classic()
risk_env_ext = risk_env_ext %>%
  mutate(env_label_faceted = paste(env_label, clust, sep = "_"),
         env_label_faceted = fct_reorder(env_label_faceted, Estimate))
risk_ext = ggplot(risk_env_ext, aes(x = env_label_faceted, y = Estimate)) + geom_point(color = "darkred") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * Std..Error, ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * Std..Error),
                width = 0.2, color = "darkred") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("_.*", "", x)) +
  facet_wrap(~ clust, scales = "free") + coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Environmental Variable", y = "Effect on Residual Externalizing Symptoms") + theme_classic()

risk = ggarrange(risk_int, risk_ext, nrow=2)
res = ggarrange(resilience_int, resilience_ext, nrow=2)
risk

res

Brain-bpm moderation

dmnfpn_2waymod = lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights)
salfpn_2waymod = lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights)

test(emtrends(dmnfpn_2waymod, var = "dmn_fpn", specs = "clust"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11413' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11413)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11413' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11413)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
##  clust dmn_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             1.211 0.564 Inf   2.147  0.0318
##  2            -1.788 1.100 Inf  -1.623  0.1047
##  3            -0.767 1.050 Inf  -0.729  0.4658
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic
test(emtrends(salfpn_2waymod, var = "sal_fpn", specs = "clust"))
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 11409' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 11409)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 11409' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 11409)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
##  clust sal_fpn.trend    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  1             0.707 0.568 Inf   1.246  0.2127
##  2             4.163 1.120 Inf   3.732  0.0002
##  3            -2.456 1.040 Inf  -2.358  0.0184
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: mri_info_manufacturer 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic
summary(dmnfpn_2waymod)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage +  
##     (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) +  
##     mri_info_manufacturer
##    Data: agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age))
## Weights: weights
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: Inf
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.4526 -0.4653 -0.1842  0.3062  7.1836 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups         Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  src_subject_id (Intercept) 2.17149  1.4736  
##  site_id_l      (Intercept) 0.02907  0.1705  
##  Residual                   0.62592  0.7911  
## Number of obs: 11413, groups:  src_subject_id, 7144; site_id_l, 22
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                                Estimate Std. Error         df
## (Intercept)                                   2.630e+00  4.602e-01  1.707e+03
## dmn_fpn                                       1.211e+00  5.640e-01  1.123e+04
## clust2                                        7.577e-01  8.983e-02  8.884e+03
## clust3                                        5.457e-01  8.685e-02  6.537e+03
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                    -1.169e-01  3.844e-02  5.915e+03
## changeage                                     3.723e-02  1.150e-01  2.586e+03
## rsfmri_meanmotion                             1.546e-01  1.026e-01  1.093e+04
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems -2.641e-01  1.528e-01  7.406e+00
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                 -1.118e-01  1.063e-01  6.793e+00
## dmn_fpn:clust2                               -2.999e+00  1.235e+00  1.133e+04
## dmn_fpn:clust3                               -1.978e+00  1.190e+00  1.135e+04
##                                              t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                                    5.714 1.30e-08 ***
## dmn_fpn                                        2.147  0.03181 *  
## clust2                                         8.435  < 2e-16 ***
## clust3                                         6.284 3.52e-10 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                     -3.040  0.00238 ** 
## changeage                                      0.324  0.74622    
## rsfmri_meanmotion                              1.507  0.13193    
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems  -1.728  0.12530    
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                  -1.052  0.32877    
## dmn_fpn:clust2                                -2.427  0.01522 *  
## dmn_fpn:clust3                                -1.662  0.09650 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) dmn_fp clust2 clust3 a___1_ changg rsfmr_ m__PMS m__SIE
## dmn_fpn     -0.048                                                        
## clust2      -0.033  0.296                                                 
## clust3      -0.052  0.310  0.213                                          
## ag_bs__1__1 -0.834 -0.005 -0.009  0.004                                   
## changeage   -0.515 -0.005 -0.001  0.008  0.006                            
## rsfmr_mnmtn -0.101 -0.074 -0.015 -0.027  0.095 -0.030                     
## mr_nf_mnPMS -0.088 -0.013  0.025  0.019 -0.013 -0.031 -0.018              
## mr__SIEMENS -0.112 -0.007  0.015  0.022 -0.030 -0.056 -0.015  0.499       
## dmn_fpn:cl2  0.016 -0.453 -0.679 -0.141  0.008  0.007  0.002  0.011 -0.001
## dmn_fpn:cl3  0.031 -0.468 -0.141 -0.671 -0.007  0.002 -0.001  0.003  0.008
##             dmn_:2
## dmn_fpn           
## clust2            
## clust3            
## ag_bs__1__1       
## changeage         
## rsfmr_mnmtn       
## mr_nf_mnPMS       
## mr__SIEMENS       
## dmn_fpn:cl2       
## dmn_fpn:cl3  0.214
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## Gradient contains NAs
summary(salfpn_2waymod)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage +  
##     (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) +  
##     mri_info_manufacturer
##    Data: agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(!is.na(age))
## Weights: weights
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: Inf
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.4738 -0.4645 -0.1839  0.3082  7.2030 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups         Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  src_subject_id (Intercept) 2.16898  1.4727  
##  site_id_l      (Intercept) 0.02891  0.1700  
##  Residual                   0.62545  0.7909  
## Number of obs: 11409, groups:  src_subject_id, 7142; site_id_l, 22
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                                Estimate Std. Error         df
## (Intercept)                                   2.670e+00  4.594e-01  1.660e+03
## sal_fpn                                       7.072e-01  5.676e-01  1.130e+04
## clust2                                        6.381e-01  6.655e-02  6.093e+03
## clust3                                        4.330e-01  6.469e-02  3.030e+03
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                    -1.154e-01  3.844e-02  5.894e+03
## changeage                                     4.210e-02  1.150e-01  2.514e+03
## rsfmri_meanmotion                             1.415e-01  1.026e-01  1.090e+04
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems -2.617e-01  1.526e-01  7.083e+00
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                 -1.117e-01  1.061e-01  6.485e+00
## sal_fpn:clust2                                3.456e+00  1.247e+00  1.136e+04
## sal_fpn:clust3                               -3.163e+00  1.183e+00  1.131e+04
##                                              t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                                    5.812 7.40e-09 ***
## sal_fpn                                        1.246  0.21277    
## clust2                                         9.588  < 2e-16 ***
## clust3                                         6.695 2.57e-11 ***
## age_baseline_year_1_arm_1                     -3.003  0.00269 ** 
## changeage                                      0.366  0.71431    
## rsfmri_meanmotion                              1.379  0.16794    
## mri_info_manufacturerPhilips Medical Systems  -1.715  0.12958    
## mri_info_manufacturerSIEMENS                  -1.053  0.32995    
## sal_fpn:clust2                                 2.771  0.00559 ** 
## sal_fpn:clust3                                -2.673  0.00753 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) sl_fpn clust2 clust3 a___1_ changg rsfmr_ m__PMS m__SIE
## sal_fpn      0.001                                                        
## clust2      -0.032 -0.049                                                 
## clust3      -0.042 -0.048  0.221                                          
## ag_bs__1__1 -0.835  0.023 -0.004 -0.001                                   
## changeage   -0.516 -0.015  0.006  0.012  0.006                            
## rsfmr_mnmtn -0.104 -0.076 -0.021 -0.036  0.092 -0.029                     
## mr_nf_mnPMS -0.089 -0.026  0.044  0.028 -0.015 -0.030 -0.015              
## mr__SIEMENS -0.112 -0.014  0.019  0.036 -0.031 -0.055 -0.013  0.499       
## sl_fpn:cls2 -0.006 -0.447  0.135  0.022 -0.001  0.010 -0.009  0.001 -0.004
## sl_fpn:cls3 -0.001 -0.475  0.022  0.096  0.002 -0.005  0.010 -0.004 -0.011
##             sl_f:2
## sal_fpn           
## clust2            
## clust3            
## ag_bs__1__1       
## changeage         
## rsfmr_mnmtn       
## mr_nf_mnPMS       
## mr__SIEMENS       
## sl_fpn:cls2       
## sl_fpn:cls3  0.215
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## Gradient contains NAs
# get predicted scores
pred_dmnfpn = ggpredict(dmnfpn_2waymod, terms = c("dmn_fpn [all]", "clust"))
pred_salfpn = ggpredict(salfpn_2waymod, terms = c("sal_fpn [all]", "clust"))

# plot predicted lines for each cluster
dmnfpn_2wayint = plot(pred_dmnfpn, show_title=FALSE, line_size=1) + labs(x = "DMN-FPN Functional Connectivity", y = "Predicted Internalizing Score") +
  theme_minimal() + theme_classic() + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(legend.position="none") + geom_rug(alpha = 0.3, position = position_jitter(width = 0.02, height = 0))
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
salfpn_2wayint = plot(pred_salfpn, show_title=FALSE, line_size=1) + labs(x = "SAL-FPN Functional Connectivity", y = "Predicted Internalizing Score") +
  theme_minimal() + theme_classic() + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme(legend.position="none") + geom_rug(alpha = 0.3, position = position_jitter(width = 0.02, height = 0))
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
# combine
ggarrange(salfpn_2wayint, dmnfpn_2wayint, nrow=2, ncol=1)

# 3 way interaction with age
dmnfpn_mod = lmer(bpm_int ~ dmn_fpn * age * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, (agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(is.na(age)==F)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights)
salfpn_mod = lmer(bpm_int ~ sal_fpn * age * clust + age_baseline_year_1_arm_1 + changeage + (1 | src_subject_id) + rsfmri_meanmotion + (1 | site_id_l) + mri_info_manufacturer, (agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(is.na(age)==F)), na.action = na.exclude, weights = weights)

# plot by cluster
dmnfpn_int = interact_plot(dmnfpn_mod, pred=dmn_fpn, modx=clust, mod2=age, data=(agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(is.na(age)==F)), interval=T, rug=T, colors=custom_colors, line.thickness = 0.75) + 
  theme_classic() + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 4)) + theme(legend.position = "none") + labs(x = "DMN-FPN Functional Connectivity", y = "Predicted Internalizing") + scale_linetype_manual(values =c("solid","solid","solid")) 
## Scale for linetype is already present.
## Adding another scale for linetype, which will replace the existing scale.
salfpn_int = interact_plot(salfpn_mod, pred=sal_fpn, modx=clust, mod2=age, data=(agnes_res_env_brainbpm_long %>% filter(is.na(age)==F)), interval=T, rug=T, colors=custom_colors, line.thickness = 0.75) + 
  theme_classic() + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 4)) + theme(legend.position = "none") + labs(x = "SN-FPN Functional Connectivity", y = "Predicted Internalizing") + scale_linetype_manual(values =c("solid","solid","solid")) 
## Scale for linetype is already present.
## Adding another scale for linetype, which will replace the existing scale.
ggarrange(salfpn_int, dmnfpn_int, nrow=2, ncol=1)
